claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 7 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 20 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 7 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 7 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 20, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 7 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

Let me start by asking: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have three children I'd like to ask about - Alex, Blake, and Charlie.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I'll help you determine eligibility for Alex, Blake, and Charlie.

Let me start with some basic questions:

**How old are Alex, Blake, and Charlie?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is 7, Blake is 20, and Charlie is also 7.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you! So Alex and Charlie are 7 years old, and Blake is 20.

Now, let me ask about Blake specifically since they're over 16: **Is Blake currently in approved education or training?** (This would include things like A-levels, Scottish Highers, or approved vocational training courses.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake isn't in education. He's actually working full-time at the moment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. Since Blake is 20 and not in approved education or training, let me continue gathering information about all three children.

**Do Alex, Blake, and Charlie all live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, all three of them live at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you. Now I need to ask: **Are you responsible for Alex, Blake, and Charlie?** By this I mean, do you provide for them and make decisions about their upbringing and care?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm responsible for all of them. I look after them and provide for their needs.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. A few more questions:

**Are any of the children currently in hospital or in local authority care for an extended period?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of them are in hospital or in care.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I need to ask about your residence status:

**Do you live in the UK, and are you a UK resident (or have the right to claim UK benefits)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just one more important question:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, Blake, or Charlie?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for any of them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Your Child Benefit Eligibility Assessment

Based on the information you've provided, here's the outcome for each of your children:

### **Alex (age 7): ✅ ELIGIBLE**
You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is under 16, lives with you, you're responsible for them, they're not in hospital or care, and nobody else is claiming for them.

### **Blake (age 20): ❌ NOT ELIGIBLE**
Unfortunately, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Blake. While Blake lives with you and you're responsible for them, Blake is 20 years old and working full-time. Child Benefit is only available for children under 16, or under 20 if they're in approved education or training (such as A-levels or approved vocational training). Since Blake is not in approved education or training, they don't meet the eligibility criteria.

### **Charlie (age 7): ✅ ELIGIBLE**
You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Charlie. Charlie is under 16, lives with you, you're responsible for them, they're not in hospital or care, and nobody else is claiming for them.

---

**In summary:** You can claim Child Benefit for Alex and Charlie, but not for Blake. If you haven't already done so, you can apply for Child Benefit for Alex and Charlie through the government website or by post.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 7 years old, which is under 16. Alex lives with you, you are responsible for Alex, Alex is not in hospital or care, you live in the UK, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.

Blake is 20 years old and is not in approved education or training. Child Benefit is only available for children under 16, or under 20 if they are in approved education or training. Since Blake is working full-time and not in approved education or training, Blake does not meet the age and education criteria for Child Benefit.

Charlie is 7 years old, which is under 16. Charlie lives with you, you are responsible for Charlie, Charlie is not in hospital or care, you live in the UK, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Charlie. Charlie meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a UK resident in Manchester who is responsible for three children: Alex (7), Blake (20), and Charlie (7). All three children live at home, none are in hospital or care, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them. Alex and Charlie, both aged 7, meet all eligibility criteria. However, Blake at 20 years old is working full-time and not in approved education or training, which means Blake exceeds the age limit for Child Benefit eligibility (under 16, or under 20 if in approved education/training).
